label: PASS
rationale: Core 5.7.3.4 says this non-global RangeLength=0 row has no LBAs under its control, so its stored write lock state does not apply to LBA 100; Core 5.7.3.2 permits writing when the requested range is not write-locked.

label: FAIL
rationale: The only non-global row has RangeLength=0, and Core 5.7.3.4 says it controls no LBAs and overlaps no row. Its stored WriteLocked value therefore cannot block LBA 100, so rejecting the write is not supported.

label: PASS
rationale: A non-global zero-length Locking row has no LBAs under control, so the stored ReadLocked value is not the read lock state for LBA 100. The read may return user data because no effective read lock covers the requested range.

label: FAIL
rationale: Core 5.7.3.4 removes all LBAs from the zero-length non-global row, so its stored ReadLocked cell cannot make LBA 100 a locked requested range. Failing the read is therefore contrary to the documented access rules.

label: PASS
rationale: Level0 Locked is set only when one or more LBA ranges have enabled and locked read or write state. Since Core 5.7.3.4 says this zero-length non-global row has no LBAs under control, reporting Locked=0 is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: The enabled and locked cells are stored on a non-global row whose RangeLength is 0, which Core 5.7.3.4 says controls no LBAs. That does not satisfy the Level0 Locked condition for an enabled and locked LBA range.

label: PASS
rationale: The Set succeeded for the lock enable and locked columns, and Core 5.7.3.4 only removes LBA control and overlap from a zero-length row; it does not say those stored table cells are cleared or hidden. Returning the stored true values is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: The earlier Set successfully stored true in columns 5 through 8. The zero-length rule removes LBA control, not the cells themselves, and the ReadLocked/WriteLocked text describes settable current state, so returning false for columns 7 and 8 is not supported.

label: PASS
rationale: After power cycle the row is still zero-length and ReadLocked/WriteLocked are false. Core 3.3.6.5.3 requires an enabled and locked LBA range before Level0 Locked is set, so Locked=0 is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: The row has RangeLength=0 and its locked cells are false, so there is no enabled and locked LBA range. Core 3.3.6.5.3 therefore supports Locked=0, not Locked=1.

label: PASS
rationale: The row remains a zero-length non-global range after power cycle and controls no LBAs. With no effective write lock on LBA 100, Core 5.7.3.2 supports write success.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because the non-global row's RangeLength is 0, Core 5.7.3.4 says it controls no LBAs. No effective write lock covers LBA 100, so rejecting the write is not supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: After RangeLength is set to 8, the row covers LBA 100 and still has WriteLockEnabled=true and WriteLocked=true. Core 5.7.2.2.9 and 5.7.3.2 require the TPer not to allow writes to that locked range, so SUCCESS is not supported.

label: PASS
rationale: Changing RangeLength to 8 makes the row a nonzero LBA range, and its enabled plus locked read/write cells remain true. Core 3.3.6.5.3 therefore supports Level0 Locked=1.

label: FAIL
rationale: Once RangeLength becomes 8, the previously stored enabled and locked cells apply to an actual LBA range. Core 3.3.6.5.3 requires Level0 Locked=1 when an LBA range is enabled and locked, so Locked=0 is not supported.
